oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Effect of Proposed Animal Welfare Regulations for Birds on Aviculturists
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Clubb, Susan
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 49 Number 3 (April 2022).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.Effect of the Proposed Animal Welfare RegulationsFor Birds on Aviculturists by Dr. Susan Clubb, DVM, DABVP, AvianNote: The comment period has been extended until May 21, 2022The Animal Welfare Act (AWA) was signed into law on August 24, 1966. It is the only federal law in the United States that regulates the treatment of animals in research, teaching, testing, exhibition, transport, and by dealers. The Act is enforced by USDA, APHIS, Animal Care.more than 18 months after publication of a notice of listening sessions, and publish a final rule no later than 1 year later. The proposed rule was published on Tuesday Feb 22, 2022.Click the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 49 No. 3 (2022): Watchbird April 2022; 6-7, 24
